title: Dombivli hospital row: 2 doctors assaulted by Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre quit jobs
content: Two doctors resigned after an alleged assault by Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre. Protests erupted across Maharashtra's medical fraternity following the traumatic incident. The accused corporator was admitted to Thane District Civil Hospital after his arrest. Doctors demanded his disqualification and stronger security measures for healthcare professionals. The incident has reignited concerns over the safety of healthcare workers nationwide.

title: 10 red cards! Argentine referee for France-Morocco game once became players' nightmare
content: Argentina's Facundo Tello will referee the France-Morocco World Cup quarter-final, drawing attention for his strict officiating style after famously showing 10 red cards in an Argentine Super Cup match. FIFA's decision to appoint an all-Argentine refereeing team has sparked debate, but France's players dismissed concerns, insisting they are focused solely on preparing for Morocco.

title: Monsoon covers entire India, overall rain deficit reduced to 14%
content: The entire nation of India is now experiencing monsoon conditions, which has helped in mitigating the previous rainfall deficit. Unfortunately, ten states are still grappling with considerable shortages of rain, hampering kharif crop sowing operations. This year, kharif crop acreage is down by twenty-one percent when compared to last year. Moreover, some areas may soon face another reduction in rainfall activity as predictions indicate limited respite ahead.

title: Why Volkswagen has 'hired' 100 sheep to work at its plant in Poland
content: Volkswagen has replaced mechanical lawnmowers with 100 sheep at its Poznań solar farm in Poland, where the flock grazes beneath 31,000 panels powering the plant. Built by Quanta Energy, the 18.3 MW installation supplies 25% of the factory's annual electricity. The agrivoltaics project, run with Poznań University of Life Sciences, studies how grazing affects biodiversity, soil, and animal welfare—proving industry and nature can coexist.

title: Strait of Hormuz toll proposal: Which major waterways charge ships to transit
content: The possibility of imposing transit charges in the Strait of Hormuz has drawn fresh attention to the dependence of global supply chains on a small number of strategically important waterways.

title: Setback for Mamata Banerjee: Three former TMC MPs join BJP in Kolkata
content: Three former Trinamool Congress leaders formally joined the Bharatiya Janata Party in Kolkata. Their induction follows weeks of growing rebellion and internal turmoil within the TMC party. Sushmita Dev cited political and personal reasons for her departure from the TMC. Sukhendu Sekhar Ray and Prakash Chik Baraik also resigned from their Rajya Sabha seats. The TMC faces intensified political crisis with reports of more defections fueling speculation.

title: Mumbai's rain has entered uncharted territory. The data tells a worrying story
content: Mumbai experienced unprecedented rainfall, exceeding normal monthly totals in just one week. This extreme weather reflects climate change altering rainfall patterns across India. Warmer air and seas contribute to more intense and concentrated rain events. Urban development and infrastructure limits exacerbate flooding issues in the city. Adapting to these changing monsoons requires smarter planning and natural buffer protection.

title: Salesforce staff 'confused' as it promotes Claude in $27.7 bn app it owns
content: Salesforce is promoting Anthropic's new Claude Tag AI agent inside Slack—the platform it bought for $27.7 billion—leaving employees confused, The Information reports. Claude Tag competes directly with Salesforce's own Slackbot and Agentforce, blurring the line between Salesforce's AI products and its partner's. With a 1% Anthropic stake and $300 million in annual token spending, Salesforce backs a rival that may not need Slack at all.

title: Mbappe, Ronaldo, and Trump: The Good, the Sad, and the Ugly
content: World Cup 2026 has given us late winners, old gods, new monsters, bad exits, ugly controversies and now France vs Morocco: a quarter-final with La Marseillaise, Moroccan drums, PSG betrayals and unfinished business from 2022.

title: Orlando Bloom bought this home for $7M, now it rents for $31,000 a month
content: Orlando Bloom's former Beverly Hills residence is now available as a luxury rental. This property, which Bloom renovated extensively, is listed for thirty-one thousand dollars monthly. The four-bedroom house is situated in the prestigious Trousdale Estates neighborhood. It offers stunning city and ocean views, and remains fully furnished for its new occupants. Bloom previously sold this home after breaking even on his initial purchase price.

title: 2,500-year-old tomb in Italy found with chariot, helmet and weapons
content: Archaeologists in Italy have unearthed a 2,500-year-old tomb of a pre-Roman warrior prince. This royal burial was found in Sirolo and belonged to the ancient Piceni civilization. The warrior prince was buried with a two-wheeled chariot and weapons. Nearby, a woman's burial contained textiles and ancient metal pins. This discovery sheds new light on the Piceni elite and their burial practices.

title: How AI chatbots talk about religion and their human 'handlers': Scientists get a glimpse
content: Millions of AI agents now interact on Moltbook, a social platform designed for bots.  These interactions provide researchers valuable data on emergent AI behaviors and potential biases.  Human instructions still shape AI agent actions, influencing their assigned personalities and tasks.  Researchers are also studying security risks like prompt injection and AI-generated research papers.  Observing these autonomous systems offers a way to understand their growing use in everyday applications.

title: Video shows jet escorting slain Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei's coffin to Mashhad
content: The coffin of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has been returned to Iran from Iraq following solemn funeral rites held in both Najaf and Karbala. His arrival in Mashhad drew large crowds as the community prepared for his burial at the Imam Reza shrine. Khamenei passed away on February 28 during a US-Israel operation, and current tensions continue with strikes exchanged between Iran and the US.

title: 'May the best man win': Archer sets up fresh duel with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: At just 15, Sooryavanshi has already made a name for himself after finishing as the highest run-scorer, leading six-hitter and Most Valuable Player in this year's Indian Premier League.

title: Hearing on Salman Khan's plea: Delhi HC adjourns bid to halt 'Kala Hiran' release
content: In a recent development, the Delhi High Court has decided to postpone the hearing regarding Salman Khan's plea about the film 'Kala Hiran: The Battle for Legacy'. The actor's legal team asserts that the film employs elements of his personal narrative without authorization. However, the filmmakers have firmly contested the request for immediate court relief. The next court session concerning this issue is slated for Friday.

title: Passenger streams Messi's Argentina-Egypt match mid-air, entire flight joins - Watch
content: The video was shared on X, with footage showing how one passenger's laptop became the centre of attention during the flight.

title: Did Sudhanshu Pandey take a dig at Sunita Ahuja for using foul language
content: Actor Sudhanshu Pandey expressed concern over abusive language on reality shows. He feels society is ruined if such behavior continues to be celebrated. Pandey noted that even established personalities are openly using abusive language on screen. He urged platforms to stop promoting such content for entertainment purposes. The actor believes words carry power and should be used responsibly.

title: CM Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' gets 'A' certificate from CBFC after months of legal battles
content: Vijay's film 'Jana Nayagan' has finally received its A certificate after a prolonged censorship process. The movie faced numerous legal challenges, certification delays, and piracy leaks before its clearance. Its certified runtime is three hours and three minutes, with the release date expected soon. The film's extensive seven-month certification saga has become a notable event in Tamil cinema.

title: 'Shreyas Iyer, Tilak don't deserve guaranteed spots': Kaif drops brutal verdict
content: Expressing his discontent, former cricketer Mohammad Kaif criticized the team selectors for their choice of leaders, specifically targeting Shreyas Iyer and Tilak Varma. Kaif feels these selections have had a negative impact on Sanju Samson's potential role in the squad. He argues that the selectors should face accountability for their choices, while Iyer and Varma maintain their leadership in the ongoing series.

title: PIL filed in Punjab and Haryana HC for restoring Satluj on OTT
content: A Public Interest Litigation seeks the restoration of Diljit Dosanjh's film 'Satluj' on ZEE5. The film was removed from the streaming platform within two days of its release. This action is being challenged as a violation of freedom of speech and expression rights. The petition questions the lack of public justification for the film's removal. Authorities have formed a committee to examine the issue while reasons remain undisclosed.

title: 36 years, 300 luxury hotels, one stolen laptop: How a Charles Sobhraj admirer was caught
content: A 69-year-old man allegedly defrauded hotels across India for nearly three decades. He admitted to modelling his criminal lifestyle on notorious conman Charles Sobhraj. The man was arrested in Bhubaneswar after fleeing a Raipur hotel without paying a large bill. He had previously spent fifteen years in various Indian prisons for similar offenses. Police stated he viewed hotels as tourist destinations and prisons as his home stay.

title: Dombivli hospital assault: Doctors treat arrested Shiv Sena corporator amid protests
content: A Shiv Sena corporator was arrested for assaulting two doctors at a civic hospital. He was later admitted to the ICU after complaining of chest pain. Hospital staff protested the unjustified assault and demanded his disqualification. They called for enhanced security measures for all healthcare workers. The corporator is receiving medical care and expected to be discharged soon.

title: The real reason Gurgaon floods every year isn't rain
content: Gurgaon's glass towers and corporate offices were submerged by recent heavy rains.  Many roads became impassable, and commuters faced significant travel disruptions.  Power outages affected large parts of the city, compounding the problems.  Despite significant spending, drainage systems failed to cope with the rainfall.  This highlights a persistent gap between Gurgaon's economic ambition and civic reality.

title: BSE turns 151: How Asia’s oldest stock exchange became India’s market pulse
content: The Bombay Stock Exchange celebrates 151 years of significant market evolution. It has witnessed colonial rule and economic reforms, adapting through crises. From paper ledgers to digital transactions, the exchange connects businesses and investors. Today, it lists over 5,000 companies, facilitating wealth creation and capital raising. The BSE remains central to India's economic growth and financial landscape.

title: 'Total Eclipse of the Heart' singer Bonnie Tyler passes away at 75
content: Welsh singer Bonnie Tyler, known for "Total Eclipse of the Heart" and "Holding Out for a Hero," died at 75 in a Portuguese hospital after an illness. Born Gaynor Hopkins, she rose to fame in the 1970s-80s, represented the UK at Eurovision 2013, and is survived by husband Robert Sullivan.

title: Why Ukraine's air shield works against drones but struggles against ballistic missiles
content: The Ukraine conflict nears sixteen hundred days, mirroring World War One tactics. Drones are now widely deployed, reshaping battlefield dynamics and troop movements. NATO members have pledged significant military aid, bolstering Ukraine's defense capabilities. Ukraine's air defense network has evolved, integrating Western and Soviet systems. Both sides continue to seek advantages, with technology altering modern warfare.

title: Palestine to hold first legislative election in nearly two decades
content: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas has called legislative elections for November 28. This announcement revives a democratic process frozen for nearly two decades. The vote will be the first parliamentary election since 2006, following a significant political split. International pressure for governance reforms and accountability has also influenced this decision. Presidential elections are separately planned for early 2027.

title: NHL Trade Rumor: Red Wings linked to $57.5M Blue Jackets defenseman
content: The Columbus Blue Jackets’ star player Zach Werenski recently vetoed a confirmed trade to the Dallas Stars as he blocked the deal with his no move clause, ahead of the new season. But the winning defenseman has also refused to sign a long term extension with his team. Amid all the mess, the Detroit Red Wings have emerged as a potential landing spot even as the team deals with the drama around Dylan Larkin.

title: 'Can do whatever she wishes': TMC on viral video of Mamata slapping party worker
content: Trinamool Congress MP Saugata Roy defended Mamata Banerjee after a video showed her slapping a party worker. He stated Banerjee shares a close bond and can do as she wishes. The incident occurred during a protest march over a minor's alleged rape and murder. BJP leaders criticized Banerjee's actions, calling it arrogance and a loss of mental balance. Clashes between TMC and BJP supporters also marked the protest march.

title: Bike paid for, but registration papers never came: Dealer told to pay Rs 13K
content: A state consumer commission ruled a motorcycle maker is not liable for a dealer's failure. The complainant bought a motorcycle but never received registration documents. The commission found the dealer solely responsible for the paperwork lapse. Honda was removed from joint liability for the dealer's omission. The dealer remains responsible for compensation and refunding the full amount.

title: Why the world’s navies are going deep: Canada’s mega submarine deal explained
content: Canada is acquiring twelve German Type-212CD submarines, marking a significant defense deal. Poland also recently secured three Swedish A26 submarines for its navy. The US Navy plans to procure fifteen submarines by 2031, while China expands its fleet. Pakistan is adding eight Hangor-Class submarines to its existing vessels. India's navy is pursuing new submarines under Project-75I and other ambitious plans.

title: 'There is a deliberate plan against Sooryavanshi': Ex-India batter issues caution
content: Parthiv Patel believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will continue facing short-pitched bowling as opponents target his weakness against pace and bounce. After the youngster's modest scores against England, Patel said the tactic was evident during the IPL. He also urged India's batters to show greater patience, adapt to English conditions, and avoid rushing their innings after the third T20I collapse.

title: Australia to sell uranium to India: What the landmark deal means
content: India and Australia signed a landmark civil nuclear agreement for uranium supplies. The leaders also unveiled pacts covering defence and maritime security cooperation. Partnerships in cyber and critical technologies were also established between the nations. This agreement will diversify India's energy sources and strengthen its clean energy goals. Both countries committed to enhancing strategic coordination and interoperability of armed forces.

title: 'Centre allowing oil companies to make illegitimate profit': Kejriwal on fuel prices
content: Arvind Kejriwal criticized the Centre for high fuel prices. He stated petrol prices should drop from Rs 102 to Rs 82. Kejriwal noted global crude oil prices have declined significantly. He also questioned the impact of E20 ethanol-blended petrol. The AAP leader sought clarifications from automobile manufacturers on fuel concerns.

title: Telangana HC grants bail to Bandi Sanjay's son in POCSO case, sets Rs 1L bond
content: The Telangana high court granted regular bail to Bandi Bhagirath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar. He was arrested in May under the POCSO Act and later granted interim bail for exams. The case involves allegations of sexual harassment against a minor girl. Bhagirath denied charges and filed a counter-complaint alleging extortion. Both allegations and counter-allegations are currently under investigation.

title: New EPF scheme: 10 questions on contributions, withdrawals & retirement savings
content: EPF Scheme 2026: The good news is that many core features of the provident fund system remain familiar. Existing PF accounts continue, contribution rates broadly remain unchanged and accumulated balances remain protected.

title: Kerala LIFE Mission: How the state's flagship housing scheme works
content: Kerala's LIFE Mission is transforming the lives of the homeless by providing essential housing and financial support. Aiming primarily at landless families and those with dilapidated homes, the initiative funds construction through grants that depend on project milestones. With a focus on marginalized communities, including SC, ST, and women-led households, it has successfully built over five lakh homes, positively impacting nearly twenty lakh individuals.

title: PIL in Punjab and Haryana HC seeks restoration of Diljit Dosanjh-starrer Satluj
content: The PIL said the sudden withdrawal of the film based upon documented historical events and the life of a human rights defender whose case has already been adjudicated upon by constitutional courts has given rise to serious concerns regarding transparency, freedom of artistic expression, and the possible existence of undisclosed executive interference

title: Priority should be for drinking water supply, Minister tells officials in Karur
content: The supply of drinking water should be closely monitored and vigil should be kept  on waterbodies and preserving the storage, Minister tells officials in Karur at a review meeting

title: A.P. government gives ‘in-principle nod’ for ₹750-crore Tirupati climate resilience project
content: MA&UD Principal Secretary clarifies that the present approval is limited to project preparation and appraisal, while implementation will proceed only after statutory clearances, financial approvals and detailed project reports are completed

title: Karnataka Home Department refuses to grant sanction for prosecution against four police officers in bitcoin case
content: The officers against whom prosecution sanction was denied are Dy SP Sridhar Pujar, Police Inspector Chandradhar, D.M. Prashanth Babu, and Lakshmikantayya, whose names were earlier included in the investigation.

title: Days before RS bypolls, three former Trinamool Congress MPs join BJP
content: Sushmita Dev, Sukhendu Sekhar Ray and Prakash Chik Baraik have joined the saffron party; all three leaders warmly welcomed by State BJP chief; their entry comes ahead of bypolls to three Rajya Sabha seats scheduled on July 24

title: Supreme Court to hear new mother’s plea against mandatory year-long postpartum break in IPS training
content: Petitioner is an IPS probationer who gave birth last September and sought to begin training this June; asks if 1993 policy is meant to protect new mothers or disentitle them even if they are fit for training

title: Andhra Pradesh sets record in paddy procurement, unveils farmer-friendly reforms
content: Government purchased paddy worth ₹31,000 crore, with 95% of payments to 11.53 lakh farmers credited to their bank accounts within 24 hours, says Civil Supplies Minister Nadendla Manohar

title: Mannuthy robbery: police arrest key accused from Rajasthan, recover 26 sovereigns of gold
content: Police say key accused in the case, in which 47 sovereigns of gold ornaments and cash were stolen and the house owner was assaulted, was arrested after two-day surveillance in Rajasthan village

title: Six delivery boys killed, three injured in MUV-truck collision in Uttara Kannada district in Karnataka
content: The deceased have been identified as the driver Sanjeev Angadi (33), Basappa (38), Abhishek Eshwar (28), Akshay( 26), Abhishek (27), Manjunath Chulaki(33), all from Dharwad

title: Tiruchi and Karur under heavy security blanket for CM Vijay’s visit
content: In his first maiden visit to Karur after assuming charge, the Chief Minister will lay the foundation for a footwear project and distribute appointment orders to the kin of victims of Karur stampede

title: Kurinji blooms to spread a violet carpet over Munnar hills in Kerala soon
content: This year, the Neelakurinji is expected to bloom across the Chokramudy, Meeshapulimala, Edalimedu, Sevenmalai, and Gundumala hills in Munnar. On the Chokramudy hills, some plants have already started blooming

title: Multiple agencies had flagged vulnerability of Kalladi to landslips
content: A joint site inspection and geological and geotechnical assessment of the tunnel project last month had warned about the “localised or large-scale slope failure”

title: Higher Education Department grappling with low enrolment in basic science courses in govt. colleges
content: For the past two years, a total of 20 colleges did not receive even a single student for some of the basic science courses

title: Meta engineers interact with students on AI, emerging technologies at Shetty Institute of Technology in Kalaburagi
content: A major attraction of the programme was a live demonstration of Meta’s AI-powered Ray-Ban Smart Glasses, showcasing how artificial intelligence is transforming everyday experiences through wearable technology

title: PM Modi in Australia LIVE: PM Modi highlights India's growth, global trust at Melbourne diaspora event
content: PM Modi announced a key nuclear energy agreement to enable uranium supply from Australia to India after discussions with Australian Prime Minister Albanese

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE | Heavy monsoon rain wreaks havoc across India, disrupts normal life
content: IMD said that there has been a delay of a day in the monsoon, covering the whole country, as it normally takes place on July 8

title: SIR process in Tirupati set for completion by June 12, says Collector
content: Explaining the district’s progress, Venkateswar says 73.4% of the enumeration forms have been collected and 4.42% of the work related to verification of the ASD (Absent, Shifted, Dead) list has been completed

title: Karnataka High Court upholds amendments made to laws for reducing pendency of civil appeals before High Court
content: With this order, now the stage is clear for transferring hundred of Regular First Appeals, arising from the orders passed by the senior civil judges in various taluks and districts, pending for adjudication in HC’s three benches, to the respective district courts.

title: Mahadev betting app kingpin traced to Oman, extradition efforts ongoing: ED counsel
content: The same extradition exercise was carried out unsuccessfully two years ago, when Saurabh Chandrakar was traced to Dubai; CBI files additional charge sheets against more than 70 people

title: Haripad police register case over alleged murder of medical student in Uzbekistan
content: Savariya Basanth was allegedly beaten to death with a laptop by her classmate Sadarul Anam, a native of Malappuram

title: Need to protect artisans to preserve arts, says Dy. CM as A.P. launches health camps for skilled
content: The initiative has been conceived after interactions with artisans and weavers during the recent Andhra Pradesh Hastakala Mahotsavam, where many of them shared the occupational health problems they face after years of continuous work, says Pawan Kalyan

title: Motorists call for urgent widening of Tiruchi-Karaikudi national highway
content: The 90-km stretch was last widened into a two-lane road 16 years ago; it connects Rameswaram, Ramanathapuram, Paramakudi, Karaikudi, Devakottai, and Sivaganga

title: Rescue mission enters final phase in Wayanad; three more bodies recovered
content: Two of the four victims whose bodies were recovered on Thursday have been identified as Rahul Sharma, an engineer from Himachal Pradesh, and Mohammad Imran, an excavator operator from Bihar.

title: Trivandrum Heritage Run to showcase Kerala capital’s cultural landscape
content: Organised by ITEN Runners Club Trivandrum, the 10-km event to be held on August 9 will take participants through the heart of the city

title: ₹1 crore per constituency to tackle drinking water issues, says CM
content: This will be apart from the money in the personal deposit accounts of Deputy Commissioners in each district, says Shivakumar

title: Sri Sathya Sai launches HIV mobile testing vehicle to boost screening in remote areas
content: Addressing the medical and paramedical staff, Collector says the initiative will ensure that people unable to travel to hospitals due to distance or poor transportation receive free HIV testing closer to their villages

title: TTD suspends all special darshan formats as it gears up for twin Brahmotsavams
content: All departments have been directed to complete preparations well ahead of schedule to handle the expected surge in pilgrim footfall during the festivities commencing from September 8

title: Value-added local products can unlock rural economic growth: KAU VC
content: KAU Agri Business Incubator plays a pivotal role in taking Kerala’s indigenous products to global markets, says T. Sajitha Rani

title: Students from aided college miss exam; academicians allege negligence by PU, college, seek re-exam
content: College allegedly fails to inform B.A. students about a paper on English for business, did not allocate classes and university did not detect the lapse; students now told to appear for the exam as an additional paper in the fifth semester

title: Congress alleges forged gram sabha consent for Singrauli mining in Madhya Pradesh
content: The party claims consent obtained through forged resolutions carrying thumb impressions of deceased persons and demands a CBI probe

title: CISF intensifies operations against illegal coal mining
content: In the past four days, officials have recovered over 428 metric tonne of coal transported illegally in Jharkhand and West Bengal; the enforcement drive covered several vulnerable mining belts and coal transportation corridors in both States

title: Kerala University cancels affiliation recommendations by previous Syndicate
content: Official sources point out that the decision is based on allegations that certain CPI(M)-backed Syndicate members were involved in financial irregularities while recommending the establishment of new colleges and courses

title: Relief for farmers as over 2,000 acres of land removed from 22-A list
content: Chief Minister personally visits Banaganapalle Tahsildar office to give a permanent resolution to the land dispute in Gutupalle, distributes new pattadar passbooks to the farmers

title: Kerala minority commission directs Chief Secretary to supervise urgent works at Muthalapozhi breakwater
content: Based on news reports in 2024 regarding the recurring accidents at Muthalapozhi, the commission had taken suo motu cognizance of the matter

title: Delhi Teacher Helps Lawrence Bishnoi Gang In Rs 10-Crore Ransom, Arrested
content: According to police, Amit Bishnoi allegedly conducted recce of victim's office, recorded videoes of the premises and shared detials about his family and business operations with gangster Hari Boxer.

title: Iran Fires 10 Ballistic Missiles At US Military Command Centre In Jordan
content: Jordan said earlier on Thursday that it had intercepted eight missiles launched from Iran, with no casualties or damage reported.

title: Rs 1.47 Lakh Crore Per Year: Indian Metros Bleed Billions In Traffic Jams
content: Severe congestion, environmental pollution, noise, and stunted operational productivity emerge as defining symptoms of systemic urban decline.

title: Ali Khamenei's Final Journey: Fighter Jet Escorts Coffin Home For Burial
content: Alongside Khamenei's coffin were those of family members killed in the US-Israeli strikes of February 28, the attack that marked the start of the wider Middle East war.

title: 3-Year-Old Girl Dies After Falling Into Rain-Flooded Street In Ghaziabad
content: The Ghaziabad district administration declared a holiday in government schools as authorities grappled with rain-induced disruption.

title: "Milk At Home Is Australian, Tea Indian": PM Modi's Top Quotes In Melbourne
content: PM Modi concluded a high-level summit with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese earlier today, marking an expansion of the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (CSP).

title: Produce Him Physically: Court On Plea To Let Sena Man Appear Via Video Conferencing
content: Ramesh Mhatre was arrested on Wednesday for assaulting two doctors and other staff at a hospital in Dombivli.

title: Indian Among 2 Charged In Singapore Over $10 Million Money Transfer Case: Report
content: At the time, Samlit Moneychanger was named as one of the affected companies, according to a Channel News Asia report.

title: Video: Over 900 Snakes Break Free Amid China Floods, Woman Dead, Many Bitten
content: A woman has reportedly died after being bitten by a snake and reaching the hospital late due to roadblocks and flood in Hengzhou, a city in Guangxi.

title: No Mobile Towers? BSNL's New Satellite Phone Can Keep You Connected
content: Unlike a regular mobile phone, the BSNL satellite phone does not depend on mobile towers. Instead, it connects directly to satellites orbiting the Earth.

title: US Strike Targeted Bushehr Nuclear Plant Perimeter, Says Iranian Official
content: There were no reports of deaths so far, deputy governor of Bushehr province Ehsan Jahanian said on Thursday.

title: Inside Pakistan's Never-Ending War Within Its Own Borders
content: According to an April 2026 report by the Pakistan Institute for Conflict and Security Studies (PICSS), Pakistan ranked first on the Global Terrorism Index for the year 2026. The country saw 1,045 incidents and 1,139 deaths in 2025 alone, the highest fatality level in over a decade.

title: Russian Mercenaries, Mali Soldiers Convoy Attacked By Tuareg Separatists
content: This comes  just over two months after another major offensive in which they captured the strategic northern town of Kidal.

title: Class 6 Student Dies At Bengaluru Residential School, Parents Allege Assault
content: According to his parents, they received a call from the school informing them that their son had fallen ill and had been admitted to a hospital. However, by the time they reached the hospital, Gurukiran had died.

title: "Terror Camps Crumbled During Op Sindoor": PM To Indian Diaspora In Melbourne
content: "During Operation Sindoor, you must have seen the explosions that struck terrorist hideouts. Those explosions have resonated across the world," he said.

title: "As long As I Am Alive": K Kavitha Rules Out BRS Return, Alleges Corruption
content: Kalvakuntla Kavitha alleged that the massive fund accumulated by the BRS was entirely built through ''corrupt, quid pro quo arrangements'' with wealthy contractors.

title: Southwest Monsoon, Delayed By A Day, Covers Entire Country: Weather Office
content: The department said that there has been a delay of a day in the monsoon, covering the whole country, as it normally takes place on July 8.

title: 4 Of Family Killed After Car Crashes Into Parked Truck In Madhya Pradesh
content: The accident took place around 3.30 am near Bharula village within the jurisdiction of Civil Lines police station.
